title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: Between life and death there is a library, and within that library, the shelves go on forever. Every book provides a chance to try another life you could have lived. A novel about regret, hope, and the power of choice.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship. Learn to write code that is easy to read, maintain, and extend. Essential reading for every serious software developer.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: From the Big Bang to black holes, Stephen Hawking explores the universe's biggest questions in clear, accessible language. A landmark volume in science writing.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A magical fable about following your dreams. A young shepherd named Santiago travels from Spain to Egypt in search of treasure and discovers something far more valuable.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: David Thomas, Andrew Hunt
description: Your journey to mastery in software development. From personal responsibility and career development to architectural techniques for keeping your code flexible.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: A stunning journey through the universe with one of the greatest science communicators of all time. Explore the nature of life, consciousness, and the cosmos.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A timeless classic about racial injustice and moral growth in the American South, told through the eyes of young Scout Finch. Winner of the Pulitzer Prize.

title: JavaScript: The Good Parts
author: Douglas Crockford
description: Discover the elegance hidden inside JavaScript. This book uncovers the beautiful, elegant, lightweight, and highly expressive language that lies within.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: Richard Dawkins's brilliant reformulation of the theory of natural selection. A revolutionary perspective on evolution, genes, and the nature of life.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian masterpiece about totalitarianism, surveillance, and the manipulation of truth. Orwell's vision remains as relevant and chilling as ever.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
author: Martin Kleppmann
description: The big ideas behind reliable, scalable, and maintainable systems. A deep dive into databases, distributed systems, and modern data architecture.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: How did humans go from insignificant apes to rulers of the planet? A bold, wide-ranging exploration of our species' past, present, and future.
